overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. LinkedIn has the largest following, while Bluesky sees a higher rate of engagement compared to Mastodon. The content is generally well-received, with grant announcements and research publications driving the most engagement.

contentThemes.description: Sharing updates about research findings, publications, and projects.
Announcing job openings and research opportunities for students and researchers.
Discussing methodological approaches and tools for detecting disinformation campaigns.
Announcing participation and topics of presentations at relevant conferences.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on Mastodon to boost visibility and engagement.
Utilize more visuals (images, videos) in posts across all platforms to capture attention.
Engage in more conversations within the comments sections to build community and foster discussion.
Run polls or ask direct questions to the audience to encourage interaction.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a decent engagement rate driven by research announcements and opportunities. Experiment with different content formats and timings to optimize reach.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has the lowest engagement rate. Consider re-sharing key posts from other platforms, focusing on community-building and joining relevant discussions to increase visibility.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest audience and receives significant engagement, particularly for grant announcements and job postings. Continue to leverage this platform for professional networking and sharing significant achievements.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
